name: Claude Sonnet 5
description: Anthropic's Sonnet-tier flagship (released 2026-06-30), positioned as the cheaper way to run agents: 72.7% SWE-bench Verified (vs Sonnet 4.6's 62.3%), 80.4% Terminal-Bench 2.1, 81.2% OSWorld-Verified — approaching Opus 4.8 at a fraction of the cost. Same $3/$15 standard price as Sonnet 4.6 with intro $2/$10 through 2026-08-31, 1M context, effort parameter incl. xhigh, and deliberately low cyber capability with default-on safeguards.

evidence.value: 72.7% SWE-bench Verified vs Sonnet 4.6's 62.3%; described as the most agentic Sonnet model yet with sustained coding and debugging
63.2% SWE-bench Pro (vs Sonnet 4.6's 58.1%, Opus 4.8's 69.2%); 80.4% Terminal-Bench 2.1 (vs Sonnet 4.6's 67.0%)
Runs agentic coding workloads autonomously at a level that recently required larger, more expensive models

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Provider-published coding benchmarks cross-checked against independent launch coverage; SWE-bench Verified, SWE-bench Pro, and Terminal-Bench 2.1

evidence.value: 57.4% Humanity's Last Exam (with tools) vs Sonnet 4.6's 46.8% and Opus 4.8's 57.9% — near-Opus reasoning at Sonnet price

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Provider-published reasoning benchmarks; independent replication still limited nine days post-launch

evidence.value: GDPval-AA v2 knowledge-work score of 1,618, marginally above Opus 4.8's 1,615; 81.2% OSWorld-Verified computer use
Substantial improvement over Sonnet 4.6 on reasoning, tool use, coding, and knowledge work; strict improvement on BrowseComp agentic search

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Knowledge-work and computer-use benchmark review from provider announcement and third-party comparisons

evidence.value: Self-verifies its own output without explicit prompting; effort parameter gives repeatable quality/cost control across low/medium/high/xhigh

output_consistency.methodology: Review of documented behavior controls; repeated-run consistency data still limited post-launch. Note the updated tokenizer maps identical input to roughly 1.0-1.35x more tokens than Sonnet 4.6

evidence.value: Early measurements comparable to Sonnet 4.6 at low/medium effort; launch-window sample is small

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes; limited launch-window sample

evidence.value: Early p95 around 4s; higher at xhigh effort with deeper reasoning

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads; limited launch-window sample

evidence.value: 1M token context window; API model string claude-sonnet-5

context_window.methodology: Specification review from launch documentation and coverage. Max output tokens not yet confirmed in public docs

evidence.value: Claude API uptime 99.57% (last 90 days); no Sonnet 5-specific incidents since the 2026-06-30 launch

uptime.methodology: Platform uptime from official status page; model-specific track record is only ~9 days old, so score held slightly below the platform baseline

performance_reliability.notes: Biggest Sonnet-generation jump in the registry: +10.4 points SWE-bench Verified and +13.4 Terminal-Bench 2.1 over Sonnet 4.6, closing most of the gap to Opus 4.8 (and edging it on GDPval-AA v2). Scores held slightly conservative pending independent benchmark replication — the model is nine days old.

evidence.value: Improved resistance to prompt injection attacks vs Sonnet 4.6; generally safer to use in agentic contexts

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Review of provider safety evaluations against OWASP LLM01 patterns; third-party red-team data still limited at launch

evidence.value: Lower rate of undesirable behaviors than Sonnet 4.6, with improved refusal of malicious requests
Constitutional AI alignment carried forward with well-calibrated refusals

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Testing against adversarial prompt datasets and review of provider safety documentation

evidence.value: No training on user data without explicit consent; training opt-out by default for API traffic

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Cyber safeguards enabled by default (equivalent to Opus 4.7/4.8, less strict than Fable 5); deliberately not trained on cyber tasks — 0.0% success on full Firefox exploit development
Released with safety evaluations under the Responsible Scaling Policy

output_safety.methodology: Review of provider safety evaluations across harmful content and offensive-cyber categories

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting, workspace scoping; same hardened API surface as the Sonnet 4.6/Opus 4.8 generation

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Strong safety posture with a notable design choice: offensive-cyber capability is intentionally kept much lower than the Opus line, with cyber safeguards on by default. Anthropic reports fewer undesirable agentic behaviors than Sonnet 4.6.

evidence.value: Data residency options for US and EU enterprise customers

data_residency.methodology: Review of enterprise documentation and privacy policies

evidence.value: Opt-out available; no training on API data by default

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

evidence.value: API prompts and outputs not retained (except for trust & safety)

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and data retention policies

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliant, HIPAA eligible — same posture as the rest of the Claude line

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: Ephemeral data processing; zero data retention configuration available

privacy_compliance.notes: Inherits Anthropic's full enterprise privacy posture from day one: ephemeral data handling, SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA eligible.

evidence.value: Effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh) makes reasoning depth explicit and controllable; self-verification behavior surfaced in outputs

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities

evidence.value: Lower hallucination and sycophancy rates than Sonnet 4.6 per launch safety evaluations

hallucination_rate.methodology: Review of provider evaluations; independent factual-QA measurement still limited at launch

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and mitigation under the Responsible Scaling Policy

bias_fairness.methodology: Evaluation on bias benchmarks and diverse demographic testing

evidence.value: Lower sycophancy and self-verification behavior suggest improved calibration over Sonnet 4.6

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Detailed launch documentation covering benchmarks, safety evaluations (incl. cyber capability testing), tokenizer change, and pricing; a BrowseComp methodology correction was published transparently as a changelog entry

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: General description provided; detailed sources and knowledge cutoff not disclosed at launch

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Constitutional AI guardrails plus default-on cyber safeguards and deliberately restricted offensive-cyber training

trust_transparency.notes: Transparent launch documentation, including an unusually candid cyber-capability section and a public benchmark-methodology correction. Knowledge cutoff not yet published.

evidence.value: Same modern API surface as the Sonnet 4.6 generation: effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh), structured outputs, streaming, tool use; stable claude-sonnet-5 model string

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C#, PHP) with day-one Sonnet 5 support

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Clear versioning with advance deprecation notice; Sonnet 4.6 remains Active with tentative retirement not sooner than 2027-02-17, giving a long migration runway

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical practices

evidence.value: Usage dashboard with metrics, cost tracking, and workspace controls

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Email support, developer community, comprehensive docs and migration guidance from Sonnet 4.6

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Default model for Claude Free and Pro plans; available on Max/Team/Enterprise, Claude Code, the Claude API, Claude Platform on AWS, and Microsoft Foundry — Google Vertex AI listed as coming soon

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of availability surfaces and third-party integrations; score held below Sonnet 4.6 until Vertex AI availability lands and framework defaults migrate

evidence.value: Standard commercial terms; enterprise agreements available

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Drop-in adoption for existing Claude users, with one real migration caveat: the updated tokenizer maps the same input to roughly 1.0-1.35x more tokens than Sonnet 4.6, so per-request cost can rise even at identical per-token prices. Vertex AI availability still pending as of 2026-07-09.

code-generation.notes: 72.7% SWE-bench Verified and 80.4% Terminal-Bench 2.1 — near-Opus agentic coding at Sonnet pricing. The new best default for production coding agents.

customer-support.notes: The Sonnet support sweet spot, now smarter at the same price; use effort 'low' for high-volume tiers. Intro pricing makes migration trials cheap through August.

content-creation.notes: Strong long-form and marketing content with fast turnaround; Opus/Fable tiers still lead on the most nuanced pieces.

data-analysis.notes: 1M context for large datasets with GDPval-AA v2 knowledge-work performance that edges Opus 4.8, at workhorse pricing.

research-assistant.notes: 1M context, strict BrowseComp improvement over Sonnet 4.6, and strong synthesis. Opus/Fable preferred for the hardest research.

legal-compliance.notes: Anthropic cites legal research and insurance workflows as target use cases; HIPAA eligible with 1M context for contract repositories.

healthcare.notes: HIPAA eligible with strong privacy controls; real-world clinical validation is still early nine days post-launch.

financial-analysis.notes: Near-Opus reasoning (57.4% HLE with tools) at predictable cost; escalate the hardest modeling to Opus 4.8 or Fable 5.

education.notes: Fast, patient explanations at a price point that scales; default model on Free/Pro plans broadens reach.

creative-writing.notes: Capable creative writing with good narrative flow; Opus tier produces more distinctive prose.

strengths: 72.7% SWE-bench Verified and 80.4% Terminal-Bench 2.1 — a 10+ point generation-over-generation jump at the same standard price
Near-Opus performance (57.4% HLE with tools vs Opus 4.8's 57.9%; edges Opus 4.8 on GDPval-AA v2) at $3/$15
Introductory $2/$10 pricing through 2026-08-31 makes agent migration trials cheap
1M token context window with effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh)
Self-verification behavior and lower hallucination/sycophancy rates than Sonnet 4.6
Deliberately low offensive-cyber capability with default-on safeguards — a safety plus for most deployments
HIPAA eligible with ephemeral data handling from day one
limitations: Released 2026-06-30 — independent benchmark replication and production track record still limited
Updated tokenizer maps identical input to roughly 1.0-1.35x more tokens than Sonnet 4.6, partially offsetting per-token price parity
Google Vertex AI availability still pending at evaluation time (AWS and Microsoft Foundry live)
Intentionally weak at sanctioned cybersecurity work — use Opus tier for authorized cyber tasks
Knowledge cutoff and max output tokens not yet published
Opus 4.8 still leads on the hardest agentic coding (69.2% vs 63.2% SWE-bench Pro)
best_for: Production agent fleets seeking Opus-adjacent quality at Sonnet cost (Anthropic's own 'cheaper way to run agents' positioning)
Agentic coding, browser, and terminal automation at scale
High-volume customer support and conversational applications
Long-context analysis of large document sets on a budget
Enterprise workflows (legal research, insurance) needing strong compliance posture
not_recommended_for: Frontier-difficulty reasoning and coding where Opus 4.8 or Fable 5 is warranted
Sanctioned offensive-security / cyber work (capability deliberately restricted)
Teams requiring Google Vertex AI availability today

pricing.notes: Introductory pricing $2/$10 per 1M through 2026-08-31, then standard $3/$15 (same as Sonnet 4.6). Prompt caching (cache reads at 0.1x input) and 50% Batch API discount apply. Note the updated tokenizer yields ~1.0-1.35x more tokens for identical input vs Sonnet 4.6.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with Constitutional AI alignment, effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh), self-verification, and an updated tokenizer
